text: Code refactoring is the process of improving the internal structure of existing code without changing its external behavior. The main goal is to make the code cleaner, easier to understand, and simpler to maintain. For example, you might rename unclear variables, split a long function into smaller parts, or remove duplicate code. Refactoring does not add new features or fix bugs—it focuses on readability and reducing technical debt. Technical debt is like a loan: if you ignore messy code now, it becomes harder and more expensive to change later. Regular refactoring keeps your code healthy and saves time in the long run.
